routingPolicy: Active docs consume the global Codex multi-agent lane contract. Current direct user policy routes every new or resumed Codex-owned worker to gpt-5.6-sol at medium effort by default, permits higher Sol effort for harder work, and forbids Terra/Luna fallback or lower effort without a newer direct user instruction.
currentFocus: Keep Retry 11 failed and unpublished while its exact CDB/target exit-status contract is resolved; then align local-retail presentation, Player Mods/Lab, and Ghidra closeouts.

currentDocsTruth.release: v1.0.9 remains the latest published unsigned portable WinUI ZIP with 949 packaged Lore documents; this source slice does not publish a release.
currentDocsTruth.product: WinUI 3 is the primary preservation/modding GUI. The separate GPL rebuild has a deterministic Core/headless verifier and one playable procedural Godot .NET First Flight client; Electron, WPF, and the old Python GUI/CLI remain archived references.
currentDocsTruth.winUiFirstUse: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and its byte-identical Lore mirror record the verified three-state setup hierarchy, preservation of temporarily unavailable remembered folders, manual Save Lab availability, shell status/focus ownership, Level 1 headings, compact wrapped source actions, truthful launch/Admin Level preset state, and demand-loaded Media VLC behavior.
currentDocsTruth.filesystemSafety: Save/options, AppCore copied-executable patching, and generated asset/package outputs use bounded roots, guarded or staged writes, final verification, recoverable full-file backups, and explicit same-user post-finalization limits.
currentDocsTruth.patchContract: The normative contract accounts for 29 catalog rows, 20 visible options, 9 hidden companions, 17 dependency edges, and 118 conflict edges. AppCore accepts only exact pinned mutation rows, publishes backup/checksum/apply/restore files atomically, and repairs damaged or truncated copies only from an integrity- and provenance-verified full-file backup. The normal Windowed & Mods flow now shows exact compatibility/recovery plus two initially unselected bounded Player Mods: PATCHED identity marker and non-save-awarding Goodies wall preview. Retained experimental and legacy recipes are grouped in a collapsed Lab. No patch bytes changed. Standalone Python patch scripts remain lab helpers without the AppCore atomic-publication guarantee.
currentDocsTruth.re: reverse-engineering/ is canonical. The 2026-07-12 crosswalk corrects 0x004081c0 to CBattleEngine__Move, 0x00410c50 to CBattleEngineJetPart__Move, and four adjacent JetPart helpers. The 6411/6411 metric is metadata closure, not semantic completeness. Active historical probes/mutators that can reapply withdrawn CMonitor ownership remain a tooling follow-up. A dedicated user-owned Codex task owns Ghidra full-reaudit absorption, recovery, retention, correction review, and targeted revalidation; its findings remain lane-owned until reviewed integration.
currentDocsTruth.runtimeObserver: The approved design progresses from an exact-specimen Level 850 identity canary to controlled raw-state observation and then Level 100 corroboration. Tasks 1-4 provide PE32-safe fingerprints, strict sanitized materialization, exact receipt-bound CDB/input identity, the locked unpatched protocol, and authority-gated fixed-role orchestration. Task 5 has eleven preserved failed runs and no published matrix. The correction integrated through f74ac902 retains the exact no-delete CDB log stream, strictly parses one bounded global `.lastevent` target process-exit event for the exact receipt-owned PID and nonzero thread, and preserves all process/module/receipt, hash, marker, managed-stop, finalized-log, and census guards. Retry 11 used fresh controls/copies/processes/proof root and passed dry run. Its live no-input log contains the exact target exit event and ordered cleanup/quit markers; managed stop succeeded, source/copy hashes were unchanged, and census was clear. It still failed closed before positive roles because CDB returned -1 while reporting the intentionally stopped target exit code 0xFFFFFFFF, and the runner currently requires CDB exit code zero before parsing. A bounded TDD investigation must derive an exact identity-bound relationship; arbitrary nonzero CDB exits remain forbidden. Stage A excludes timing, field semantics, handling, and parity claims.
currentDocsTruth.referenceCorpora: The pinned audit records Onslaught 792545b as an incomplete/non-buildable architecture corpus (110 tracked files; 202/254 quoted includes absent) and AYAResourceExtractor 6f3df296 as a Windows x86 static model/texture exporter with format, fixture, build, and component-license gaps. Older 111/75 manifests counted .git control files.
currentDocsTruth.lore: The 949-document package count proves packaged breadth, not editorial completeness, freshness, provenance/rights review, or broad public-safety approval.
currentDocsTruth.rebuild: The GPL-3.0-or-later RE-informed source rebuild includes the deterministic net8 Core/headless verifier, pure real-time Client adapter, and playable procedural Godot 4.7 First Flight view. Transform, energy, shield, movement, arena, and presentation choices remain synthetic. A completed external branch proposes an optional ignored local-retail mesh preview and updated Stuart reference pins; it is not current main truth until independent safety/provenance review, hardening, and integration complete. The campaign completion target is one reproducible BYO-asset, evidence-backed playable vertical slice, not full retail parity. The client is not in the published v1.0.9 WinUI ZIP.
currentDocsTruth.curatedSourcePackage: The curated artifact is a self-contained, plain non-Git convenience package over the already-public canonical repo, not a replacement checkout or a private/public legal cut. The accepted First Flight source candidate selected 721 files, materialized 726 files with export metadata, excluded the real game-generated save baseline, and passed standalone payload/inventory, docs, npm test, notices, and hygiene gates.

activeBlockers: CURRENT_CAPABILITIES.md and reverse-engineering/RE-INDEX.md remain too large for fast contributor orientation.
release/readiness/ and tools/ still need generated front-door indexes before broad movement or deletion.
Lore needs an explicit editorial allowlist, third-party-text inventory, rights/provenance review, and generated canonical projections before another release.
The grouped WinUI action/reaction map still needs a verified control-by-control refresh against the current GUI.
The movement slice still lacks copied-runtime measurements, and the AYA lane lacks a tracked public end-to-end synthetic export fixture plus resolved component-license posture.
Active BattleEngine historical probes/mutators can still reapply withdrawn CMonitor ownership; they must be retired or fail-closed before another related Ghidra mutation wave.
The AYA export orchestrator preflights only legacy AYA/DDS DLLs even though the harness also requires Fbx.dll and an FBX template.
nextSteps: Resolve Retry 11's exact CDB/target exit-status relationship with a failing disposable test and narrow identity-bound rule, then run a wholly fresh Retry 12 and publish only a complete sanitized result.
Review and harden the completed optional local-retail presentation branch before integrating its submodule pins, exporter/bootstrap path, and Godot adapter changes.
Integrate the final Ghidra closeout correction/readback commits and reconcile tracked RE/Lore truth before retiring that task and worktree.
Retire stale BattleEngine ownership tooling and add fail-fast Fbx.dll plus FBX-template preflight with focused tests.
Freeze one accepted public-safe movement/morph/camera behavior contract, implement it in deterministic Core, then continue the Lore editorial/provenance pass.
